name: Parler-TTS (Controllable Zero-Shot TTS)

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace Hub for model download

performance.notes: GPU strongly recommended; mini model is significantly faster than large; streaming supported

agent_readiness.notes: HuggingFace's controllable zero-shot TTS model. Control speaking style via text description: 'A female speaker with a slightly high-pitched voice delivers her words quite expressively, in a very confined sounding environment with clear audio quality.' No voice cloning needed. Models: parler-tts-large-v1 and parler-tts-mini-v1. Outputs 44kHz audio. Streaming generation for lower latency. Built on EnCodec audio codec. Great for content where you want expressive, natural-sounding speech without a reference audio sample.

gotchas: Text description of voice is inexact — takes experimentation
Quality varies by voice description complexity
best_when: Zero-shot TTS with text-described speaking styles
Content creation requiring varied speaker styles without voice cloning
avoid_when: Exact voice cloning (use XTTS or Coqui TTS)
summary: Parler-TTS is HuggingFace's controllable zero-shot TTS model where you describe the voice in text ('A female speaker delivering words expressively in a quiet studio'). No reference audio needed. Large and mini models. Streaming generation. 44kHz output. Non-deterministic — different runs vary. Best for content creation needing varied natural-sounding speech without voice cloning or per-speaker training.
